title: Level 694
wikitext:
{{Fix|date=June 30, 2026|1=The exit is so overcomplicated that I personally find it shocking it somehow got documented}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 694: "Juvenile Recollection"}} {{CSS|content=.theme-fandomdesktop-light, .theme-fandomdesktop-dark { --theme-page-background-color: #121212; --theme-page-background-color--secondary: #1e1e1e; --theme-page-text-color: #f0f0f0; --theme-page-accent-mix-color: #2c2c2c; --theme-community-header-color: #ffffff; --theme-link-color: #dddddd; --theme-accent-color: #3a3a3a; --theme-accent-label-color: #ffffff; --theme-border-color: #333333; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: #1e1e1e; } }} __NOTOC__ {{Blurbackground|file=Level 60 rewrite imeage!!!.jpg}} {{Class Deadzone}}
{{Headerbox|header=Party's over.|file=Level 60 rewrite imeage!!!.jpg|speed=5s}} Level 694, otherwise known as "Juvenile Recollection", is the 695th level of [[The Backrooms]]. ==Description== {{Blurimage|file=ooh very scary.jpg|caption=An image of Level 694.}} The entirety of this level presents itself as a labyrinthine soft play center, appearing as it is within closing hours. The level possesses a colossal size, with many estimating its expanse spans over five hundred thousand square kilometers in size using various methods. Level 694 also contains a variety of structures that one would expect to find in a soft play structure in '''[[The Frontrooms]]''', including swings, trampolines, slides, merry-go-rounds, and abnormally large soft play structures. The composition of the level is immensely variable; the rooms and open spaces stretch out in a multitude of directions and seem to shift sporadically, causing disorientation among individuals across Level 694. Most of the walls and ceilings appear to be made of a material that resembles concrete, painted a deep black, while the flooring generally consists of either multicolored carpeting, foam floor tiles, concrete, or wood. Many of the play structures can be described by wanderers as holding a maze-like structure that, like the rest of the level, shifts parts of itself while one focuses on a contrasting direction. Most surfaces in the play structures are covered with padding, seemingly comprised of foam covered with a layer of thick, colorful vinyl that seems impenetrable. Within the play structures, there are a variety of objects that would typically be used for play, including different types of slides, ladders, nets, ball pits, and illustrations, among many others. However, some of these are widely different from their counterparts in normal reality, with slides being exceedingly elongated, ball pits being over twenty feet deep, ladders curving, and tunnels being difficult or impossible to navigate. The vast majority of structures within Level 694 appear to be in a state of disrepair. Individuals inside this level have commonly reported coming across specimens of mold, vines, and other, unidentified plant life growing out of various surfaces. In addition, it appears that dust has gathered along the floors throughout the level over an extended period of time. This dust commonly gets blown around by even the slightest of air currents, causing difficulty breathing in many areas. Many of the installations in Level 694 also seem to be missing different parts, such as support beams, walls, and other aspects that would be there for safety or that one would use to get around. The environment of the level has been described by many to be off-putting. There appears to be a speaker system across the level that plays unsettling music at inconsistent intervals, giving the level an intimidating atmosphere overall. Additionally, a dense fog appears to engulf the entire level, impairing one's vision significantly. Wanderers, upon entry, may also feel an intense feeling of dread, anxiety, and hopelessness. Reports of feeling like finding salvation is an infeasible task are extremely common. The aforementioned effects, interestingly, can be temporarily cured with '''[[Object 1|Almond Water]]'''. Varieties of spacial, auditory, and visual anomalies are common to encounter. Corridors and installations in Level 694 appear to warp and curve horizontally and vertically in unnatural ways. Encountering holes that appear bottomless and objects clipping into one another are common sights as well. Visual anomalies may involve hallucinations such as one moving in impossible directions or seeing glowing eyes in the dark. Auditory anomalies can include whispering, ringing in the ears, and others of that sort. ==Entities== Encountering entities in Level 694 is unfortunately a very common scenario. These entities can include but are not limited to '''[[Entity 3|Smilers]]''', '''[[Anethikas]]''', '''[[Spines]]''', and instances of individuals infected by the '''[[Sanguine Festivus]]''' virus. ===Charlotte=== {{Blurimage|file=generic bounce house.png|caption=An image of Level 694, with Charlotte visible in the distance. Photographer missing.}} Charlotte, appearing to be this level's "mascot" entity, takes the appearance of a small, sentient teddy bear. Bearing an unsettling appearance, Charlotte's fur is a dusty, weathered gray color, giving the impression that it has been abandoned by a child owner. Her eyes appear as a jet black color, and a red cross spans across her front, presumably being bloodstains. This entity is extremely dangerous, as she is exceedingly violent to human life that she finds in Level 694. Her presence can be identified with a sudden drop in temperature and a faint sound of a lullaby in the distance. Her movements are unpredictable, as she can stand still for hours or can appear in different locations in an instant without making a sound. Using the aforementioned movement traits, Charlotte has a unique attack method. At first, she appears to stalk wanderers from a distance, though after an indeterminate amount of time her face will shift into an unsettling, grotesque configuration, before lunging at the victims face. The combination of extreme visual horror and the speed of her attack often results in fatal outcomes. ==Colonies and Outposts== Due to the nature of Level 694, colonies and outposts are unable to be established. ==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== *If one comes across a group of exactly fourteen balloons and pops five of them, they will be sent here. *Noclipping in any indoor level has an extremely small chance to bring you here. *Jumping from the second floor of [[Level 624]] may send one here. *Slides that phase through the ground in [[Level 369]] send one here. *Finding a door with party streamers hanging from the top of the doorframe and entering it will bring you here. This most commonly happens in '''[[Level 21]]''', '''[[Level 11.6]]''', and '''[[Level 40]]'''. ===Exits=== Exiting this level is an extremely arduous task, as it requires a sequence of precise steps that one will only have one chance to carry out.
'''Open Attached File >'''

694exits.txt
''Exit Guide''

If one wishes to exit Level 694, they must follow these '''exact''' steps, provided below.




*Look for a space that bears the exact appearance of the one taken in the image below. [[File:1_KA_BEM080822_wacky_08.webp|thumb|469px|center]] *Look at the floor. After approximately one minute, if you feel a headache, then sit down and close your eyes. If you do not feel a headache, you are at the wrong location. *If you hear wind blowing behind you, you may get up. Whatever is controlling it likes to take its time. ''Be patient''. *Once you get up, immediately jump into the ballpit and look for a slide buried beneath the balls. *Go down the slide. If you have done the previous steps correctly, you should be inside of this room: [[File:Screenshot 2025-05-29 150704.png|thumb|469px|center]] *Immediately examine the room. If there is a light switch, then you will not make it out of this level. *If there is not a light switch, go up the staircase. Do not explore the downstairs area; whatever inhabits it doesn't like visitors. *At the top of the staircase, there should be a set of five doors. Look up. If the ceiling is clean, enter the third door. If it is dirty, enter the second. *Walk down the hallway that the door opens up to. Do not look behind you. *When the hallway starts seeping blood, turn around and run. Enter the door you came from, which will lead you to the room below. [[File:almost there?.webp|thumb|469px|center]] *Make your way across the ballpit without falling in. There is something in there. *Enter the door at the end of the hallway, which will lead to [[Level 695]].





This method can only be used once. If you find yourself re-entering this level, there is no escape. Accept whatever fate awaits you.





































Because it has already accepted you.
